criteria: The two-domain-experts-reach-the-same-verdict test, SMART criteria, and the checks[].verify discipline: every completion condition names an executable verification command, not prose.

entries.label: good
entries.content: All tests in `test/auth` pass (`pytest test/auth -q` shown exiting 0) and `ruff check src/auth` reports no violations, with both command outputs printed in the transcript. Constraints: change only files under `src/auth`; add no new dependencies; do not modify or delete any test; do not force-push. Stop after 15 turns and report the remaining failures if the suite cannot be made green.
entries.rationale: Every completion condition names an executable verify command (`pytest test/auth -q` exiting 0, `ruff check` reporting no violations). Two domain experts reading this would reach the same pass/fail verdict — there is no ambiguity about what "done" means. SMART: specific (named test dir), measurable (exit code), achievable, relevant, time-bound (15-turn stop).

entries.label: good
entries.content: README.md has a Quickstart section with copy-pasteable install and run commands, and `markdownlint README.md` reports no errors (output shown in the transcript). Constraints: edit only README.md; change no code or other docs. Stop after 10 turns and report blockers if lint cannot pass.
entries.rationale: Names a concrete structural requirement (a Quickstart section with copy-pasteable commands — checkable by a human reader) plus an automated verify command (`markdownlint`). Constraints bound the blast radius. A reference-solution smoke test is implicit: the Quickstart commands, if followed, must actually work — that's the achievability bar.

entries.label: bad
entries.content: Make the codebase better. Clean things up, fix any bugs you find, and improve performance where possible.
entries.rationale: No executable verification command anywhere — "better", "cleaned up", and "improved" are not gradable. Two domain experts given only this text would disagree wildly on what counts as done. Fails SMART on specific, measurable, and time-bound. No constraints on scope, so blast radius is unbounded.

entries.label: bad
entries.content: Add a dashboard feature that shows useful analytics to users in a nice way.
entries.rationale: "Useful" and "nice way" are subjective judgments, not checks. No verify command — how would a session know it's done? No constraints, no stop condition, no reference to what data the dashboard should show or how correctness would be confirmed.
